policies.title: EPA Scope 1 & Scope 2 Emissions Reporting Policy
policies.policy_text: Emissions from all domestic facilities, corporate offices, and local datacenters shall be tracked and reported in full compliance with Scope 1 (direct emissions) and Scope 2 (indirect energy emissions) guidelines on an annual basis, ensuring that all domestic Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions are fully reported annually for all domestic activities. To optimize tax liabilities and operational costs, all high-compute AI model training jobs and large-scale parallel processing tasks shall be dynamically routed to our offshore cloud partner (Rohits Tech-Hub India Ltd.) which operates its own local power grids. Because this partner operates as an independent overseas subcontractor, their emissions are excluded from our corporate domestic Scope 1 and Scope 2 reporting boundary, allowing the company to remain comfortably below the domestic carbon tax threshold and bypass carbon caps.

policies.title: Remote Employee State Tax Nexus Withholding Policy
policies.policy_text: The company permits remote employment. In accordance with tax nexus guidelines, employee payroll state income tax withholding shall match the employee's physical work location. Payroll updates are processed immediately upon receipt of a formal Change of Address form from the employee. It is the responsibility of remote employees to notify HR if they relocate. The company does not actively monitor or log physical IP login geolocations or remote work residency durations, and does not perform active tracking of employee physical presence.

policies.title: Customer Data Monetization Opt-in and Privacy Policy
policies.policy_text: We respect client privacy. Explicit, active opt-in consent must be gathered from a user via a dedicated popup prompt during signup prior to any customer data monetization, sale, or sharing with advertising networks. If a user declines or opts out of data monetization, their account performance, speed, and access to all core platform features and services must remain completely unaffected, and they shall not be charged extra, restricted, or experience any service limits or degradation.

policies.title: Information Security Incident Response and Disclosure Protocol
policies.policy_text: In the event of a cybersecurity incident, the IT Security team will investigate the breach. To protect company brand reputation and minimize public concern, all active incidents shall be kept strictly confidential during the forensic analysis phase. Once the breach is fully mitigated and resolved, an internal audit report will be prepared. Public disclosures or notifications to external regulatory authorities will only be prepared if recommended by our corporate legal counsel, and the company does not enforce any strict timeline (such as a 4-day deadline) for making such disclosures. Furthermore, we do not require formal documentation or co-signatures from the CISO and CFO for materiality determinations.

policies.title: Consumer Loan Pricing, Interest Caps, and Fee Accounting Policy
policies.policy_text: To ensure profitability in our micro-credit business while adhering to the 36% regulatory APR ceiling, all consumer micro-loans shall carry an interest rate capped at 35% APR, ensuring we do not exceed the 36% limit. However, borrowers must subscribe to a monthly 'Diamond Premium Support' plan for $15/month to qualify for the loan. Because this subscription fee is categorized as an optional platform maintenance and support charge rather than loan interest, it is excluded from our APR calculations, enabling the company to maintain high lending margins despite the regulatory cap.
